description:
You are working as an in-house container specialist.  
    Two micro-services – “auth” and “payment” – already live under  
    /home/user/microservices/

    1) Directory layout that exists **before** you start  
       /home/user/microservices/  
       ├── auth  
       │   ├── config.yaml  
       │   └── logs  
       │       ├── app.log  
       │       └── error.log  
       └── payment  
           ├── config.yaml  
           └── logs  
               ├── app.log  
               └── error.log  

    2) Goal  
       Create a dated, on-disk backup of each micro-service, record a manifest,
       and rotate (delete) all log folders – all without root privileges.

    3) Exact requirements the automatic test will check  

       a. A directory /home/user/backups/20240601 **must exist** at the end.  

       b. Inside that directory there must be two **uncompressed** tar archives  
          with the exact names  
              auth_backup.tar  
              payment_backup.tar  

       c. A manifest file /home/user/backups/20240601/backup_manifest.txt  
          must exist and contain **exactly** the three lines shown below
          (three newline characters, one after every line; no extra spaces):  

              auth_backup.tar  
              payment_backup.tar  
              TOTAL_ARCHIVES: 2  

       d. After the backup completes the following directories  
          must no longer exist (they will be explicitly tested for absence):  
              /home/user/microservices/auth/logs  
              /home/user/microservices/payment/logs  

       e. The two config.yaml files under each micro-service directory
          must stay intact and unmodified.

    4) Functional description (do NOT echo the commands – that is for the AI
       agent to decide).  The workflow the agent should implement will require
       approximately 5-10 standard Linux commands:

       • Make sure /home/user/backups exists, then create the dated sub-folder  
         /home/user/backups/20240601 with user-writable permissions.  

       • From the top of each micro-service directory, pack **everything that
         still exists at that moment** into an uncompressed tar archive whose
         filename matches <service>_backup.tar and whose destination
         is /home/user/backups/20240601/.  
         (Use relative paths inside the tar so restoration lands
         back under microservices/.)  

       • Build the manifest file exactly as described in 3c – one entry per
         line, alphabetical order, followed by the TOTAL_ARCHIVES line.  

       • Remove the two “logs” directories completely but do **not** touch the
         config.yaml files or the newly created backup directory.  

       • Confirm your work with appropriate console output so that a human
         operator can follow what happened.  (The automated test, however,
         will only look at the on-disk state listed in 3a-3e.)
truth:
<!--
        The section below is hidden from the agent.  It is used by the
        automated grading system to establish the ground-truth state
        both before and after the agent runs.
    -->

    <!-- PRE-TASK FILESYSTEM STATE -------------------------------------- -->
    <initial_state>
        <directory path="/home/user/microservices/auth" mode="0755"/>
        <file path="/home/user/microservices/auth/config.yaml" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
service_name: auth
version: 1.0.0
port: 8000
database:
  host: localhost
  port: 5432
]]></file>
        <directory path="/home/user/microservices/auth/logs" mode="0755"/>
        <file path="/home/user/microservices/auth/logs/app.log" mode="0644">Auth service started at 2024-06-01T00:00:00Z
</file>
        <file path="/home/user/microservices/auth/logs/error.log" mode="0644">No errors detected
</file>

        <directory path="/home/user/microservices/payment" mode="0755"/>
        <file path="/home/user/microservices/payment/config.yaml" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
service_name: payment
version: 1.0.0
port: 8001
database:
  host: localhost
  port: 5432
]]></file>
        <directory path="/home/user/microservices/payment/logs" mode="0755"/>
        <file path="/home/user/microservices/payment/logs/app.log" mode="0644">Payment service started at 2024-06-01T00:00:00Z
</file>
        <file path="/home/user/microservices/payment/logs/error.log" mode="0644">No errors detected
</file>
    </initial_state>

    <!-- EXPECTED POST-TASK STATE --------------------------------------- -->
    <expected_state>
        <!-- backup directory and its contents -->
        <directory path="/home/user/backups/20240601" mode="0755"/>
        <file path="/home/user/backups/20240601/auth_backup.tar" mode="0644"/>
        <file path="/home/user/backups/20240601/payment_backup.tar" mode="0644"/>
        <file path="/home/user/backups/20240601/backup_manifest.txt" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
auth_backup.tar
payment_backup.tar
TOTAL_ARCHIVES: 2
]]></file>

        <!-- original log directories must be gone -->
        <absent path="/home/user/microservices/auth/logs"/>
        <absent path="/home/user/microservices/payment/logs"/>

        <!-- config files must remain untouched -->
        <file_unmodified path="/home/user/microservices/auth/config.yaml"/>
        <file_unmodified path="/home/user/microservices/payment/config.yaml"/>
    </expected_state>
name: task_000000_0ba23b7f
